hero.bio: Fuelled by a passion for research, building, and teaching, I'm a Research & Teaching Assistant at Wichita State University pursuing a PhD in Electrical & Computer Engineering. Explore my journey using the links below.

intro: I'm Dhurba Sapkota, a Research & Teaching Assistant at Wichita State University, currently pursuing a PhD in Electrical & Computer Engineering.

I support students with coursework, clarify technical concepts, and assist with grading and feedback to improve learning outcomes. This role strengthens my communication, problem-solving, and mentoring skills while deepening my technical understanding.
about.careerText: A snapshot of my education and current role — building a foundation at the intersection of software engineering and security research.

timeline.title: Research & Teaching Assistant
timeline.place: Wichita State University — supporting students, clarifying concepts, grading & feedback.

work.title: User Data Deletion Verification
work.desc: A research project that verifies whether mobile applications truly delete user data from their backend servers after an account is deleted — addressing a critical privacy and data-protection concern in modern apps.

work.title: Teaching & Mentorship
work.desc: Ongoing work guiding students through engineering coursework — translating dense technical concepts into clear, approachable explanations and providing actionable feedback that improves learning outcomes.
